use_cases.work_hours: 15-2 Hours  per case depending on complexity
use_cases.nature:
UI Reading & Writing
Data Massaging
DB Access
use_cases.input_files:
1. Invoice PDF – always present.
2. Supporting Documents – optional (can be multiple, in PDF or Excel format).
3. PRF Excel File – contains details for Oracle entry (usually present).
4. Approver Master file/Email Body – treated as input since it may contain approver details. – treated as input since it may contain approver details.
use_cases.pain_areas:
1. Time Consuming 
2.  Repetitive Work
3. Manual Errors
use_cases.summary:
1.	Email Monitoring and File Download:
- Monitor a specific email ID where all invoice-related documents are received.
- Email contains:
  - PDF copy of the invoice.
  - Supporting documents (Excel/PDF) from an internal team – these vary and are not standardized.
  - An Excel file named PRF, containing invoice details required for Oracle input.
- BOT downloads all attachments.
- For invoices up to ₹1,00,000: Either Agreement or Purchase Order (PO) is mandatory.
- For invoices upto 5 lakh: Both Agreement and PO are required.
- For invoices above ₹5,00,000: Must go through Approval Matrix validation, with all necessary approval trail and authorization documents attached.
2.	Data Extraction:
- Extract required fields from the invoice PDF and supporting Excel files.
- Each vendor (approx. 3000 vendors) has a different invoice template – no standard format or file naming convention is followed.
- The first step is to verify whether the invoice is addressed to Unity Bank, as at times, invoices meant for other entities are mistakenly shared with Unity.
- Extract essential fields like: Invoice No., Supplier Name, Bank Details, Amount, GST, Invoice Date, etc.
3.	Oracle Cloud Login:
- BOT logs into Oracle Cloud using provided username and password.
- The session automatically times out after every 8 hours, requiring the user to log in again.
- An OTP received via email is required once every 24 hours.
- For the rest of the day, login can be done using only the User ID and Password.
4.	Invoice Duplication Check:
- Search using Invoice No.
- If "Result Not Found" appears, proceed with invoice upload.
- If an existing entry is found, skip the invoice and move to the next one.
5.	Invoice Upload – General Tab:
- Click Create to initiate new invoice entry.
- Enter Supplier Name.
- Select appropriate Supplier Site Location from dropdown.
- The layout auto-fills supplier-related details — cross-verify with invoice-extracted data.
6.	Bank Details Verification:
- If bank details are available on the invoice, cross-check with the supplier layout.
- Validate Account No., IFSC, etc.
7.	Invoice Classification and Entry:
- Select Invoice Type (e.g., Tax Invoice, IRN).
- In the Description tab, enter a comment like: "Payment towards advertising expense."
  (Note: This varies based on service type.)
- In Payment Terms, always select Immediate from the dropdown.
- Enter Invoice Date from the PDF.
8.	Attachment Upload:
- Click the Attachment icon and upload:
  - Invoice PDF.
  - All supporting documents received in the email.
  - The email body itself.
9.	Accounting Tab:
- Enter the GST Number of the supplier.
10.	Additional Information Tab:
- Enter the Requester’s Email ID (i.e., the internal team member who shared the invoice).
11.	Approver Details Handling:
- Approver name is usually available in the Excel file named PR Google Ads or similar (this may vary by vendor).
- If not available in the Excel, extract the approver name from the email body.
- Use the Master Approver List to identify the Department Name based on the Approver’s name.
12.	General Tab – Line Level Entry:
- Click on Line Level section.
- Enter the Base Amount.
- In Distribution Combination, input segments like:
  - Entity
  - Location Code
  - GST details
  - Any other allocation details as per invoice.
13.	Tax Calculation and Validation:
- Click the Refresh icon to auto-calculate tax.
- If the Total section shows a Green Tick, all allocations are correct.
- If an Exclamation Mark appears, it indicates an issue in allocation.
14.	Save Invoice:
- Click Save on the right side.
- A confirmation Pop-up will appear confirming the invoice is saved.
use_cases.solution_mapping: Email listener, IDP for PDF invoices, OCR, Robotic Process Automation, Oracle Cloud integration

servers:
AutomationEdge Application Server
(Main Server incl. Active MQ, PostgreSQL DB, DocEdge)

AutomationEdge Processing Server
(incl. IDP Processing Agents & Robot Sessions)
AE Main Server, DocEdge Server, Active MQ, PostgreSQL DB & Processing Server
Desktop Development Machine for Chatbot / Script Development
